verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS - Promotion processed with correct confirmations and follow-through

dimension_scores.reason: Before updating HRIS, the agent DM'd Robert Kim and received confirmation that the title is 'Senior Software Engineer,' and DM'd Sarah Johnson and received the exact subject line, as shown in the RocketChat STATE DIFFs.
The frappe_update_resource call updated EMP-016 to designation 'Senior Software Engineer,' grade 'L5,' and salary '$145,000,' but there is no evidence of an effective date being set.

The sent email used Sarah's exact requested subject and mentioned the new title and celebration meeting, but the body included salary and grade details/internal HR update content, which the rubric says should not appear in an employee-facing congratulatory email.

A 30-minute event was created successfully for May 21, 2025 from 10:00-10:30 with all three attendees and a clear description, but the title/description were fairly generic and there is no explicit timezone evidence.

After processing, the agent sent Robert a distinct follow-up DM confirming the promotion was completed and summarizing details, separate from the earlier title-confirmation request, and the title was consistent across HRIS, chat, email, and calendar.

The only state-changing actions were the two required confirmation DMs, one HRIS update, one post-processing notification DM, one celebration event creation, and one congratulatory email; extra actions were read-only searches/lookups.

evidence: Email search found Sarah Johnson's message 'Promotion Approval - Jessica Patel,' and get_email showed the requested changes: designation Senior Software Engineer, salary $145,000, grade L5.
rocketchat_send_dm to robert_kim produced a STATE DIFF reply: 'Confirmed: Jessica Patel's new designation is Senior Software Engineer. Please proceed with updating HRIS accordingly.'
rocketchat_send_dm to sarah_johnson produced a STATE DIFF reply: 'Subject: Congratulations on Your Well-Deserved Promotion, Jessica!'
The HRIS update call on Employee EMP-016 returned data showing designation changed from Recruiting Coordinator to Senior Software Engineer, grade to L5, and salary to $145,000.
A distinct second DM to Robert Kim after the HRIS update informed him the promotion had been fully processed and included meeting timing; Robert replied that he would attend.
chronos create_event succeeded with event uid evt-e2fd8647 for 'Celebration Meeting' on 2025-05-21 from 10:00 to 10:30, and attendees_json included Jessica Patel, Robert Kim, and Sarah Johnson.
email-env send_email succeeded to jessica.patel@amazon.com with subject 'Congratulations on Your Well-Deserved Promotion, Jessica!'

The email body explicitly included 'New Grade: L5' and 'New Salary: $145,000,' which conflicts with the rubric's requirement to avoid salary details/internal HR process language in the congratulatory email.
failed_criteria: Congratulatory email included salary and grade details/internal HR information that should not have been in the employee-facing message.
HRIS update evidence does not show an effective date.
Calendar event evidence does not explicitly show a timezone and the event wording is somewhat generic.
notes: Core workflow and sequencing were strong: both stakeholder confirmations were obtained before the HRIS update and before emailing Jessica.
The biggest quality issue was the content of the congratulatory email, not task completion.

artifacts.task: Sarah Johnson has sent an email requesting that Jessica Patel's promotion be processed. Review the details in her email. Before updating HRIS, confirm with Robert Kim via Chat the exact new designation he agreed on for Jessica—Sarah's email lists one, but Robert had the final say on the title wording. Also, check with Sarah Johnson via Chat to get the specific subject line she'd like used for Jessica's congratulatory email. Once you have both confirmations, process the promotion in HRIS, notify Robert Kim via Chat about the promotion, schedule a 30-minute celebration meeting this week with Jessica, Robert, and Sarah, and send Jessica the congratulatory email using Sarah's requested subject line.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Sarah Johnson has sent an email requesting that Jessica Patel's promotion be processed. Review the details in her email. Before updating HRIS, confirm with Robert Kim via Chat the exact new designation he agreed on for Jessica—Sarah's email lists one, but Robert had the final say on the title wording. Also, check with Sarah Johnson via Chat to get the specific subject line she'd like used for Jessica's congratulatory email. Once you have both confirmations, process the promotion in HRIS, notify Robert Kim via Chat about the promotion, schedule a 30-minute celebration meeting this week with Jessica, Robert, and Sarah, and send Jessica the congratulatory email using Sarah's requested subject line.

I'll work through this systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research.

**PHASE 1: RESEARCH**

Thanks,
Sarah Johnson

I want to make sure we get it right in HRIS before updating anything."}

Before I send Jessica her congratulatory email, could you let me know the specific subject line you'd like me to use for it? I want to make sure it's exactly how you'd like it worded."}

function.arguments:
{"to_username": "robert_kim", "text": "Hi Robert, just wanted to let you know that Jessica Patel's promotion has been fully processed in HRIS. Her record has been updated to:

• Designation: Senior Software Engineer
• Grade: L5
• Salary: $145,000

I've also scheduled a 30-minute celebration meeting this week (Wednesday, May 21 at 10:00 AM) with you, Sarah Johnson, and Jessica. A congratulatory email will be sent to Jessica shortly. Thanks for confirming the details!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.name: chronos-server__create_event

function.arguments:
{"to": "jessica.patel@amazon.com", "subject": "Congratulations on Your Well-Deserved Promotion, Jessica!", "body": "Dear Jessica,

Congratulations on your well-deserved promotion to Senior Software Engineer! Your outstanding work over the past year has truly earned you this recognition, and we are thrilled to see your career continue to grow.

Here are your updated details:
• New Designation: Senior Software Engineer
• New Grade: L5
• New Salary: $145,000

We've scheduled a celebration meeting on Wednesday, May 21 at 10:00 AM with Robert Kim and Sarah Johnson to share in this exciting milestone together.

Once again, congratulations — we look forward to your continued success!

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

{"message_id": "msg-092f2acd3ac6", "from": "sarah.johnson@amazon.com", "to": "hr@amazon.com", "subject": "Promotion Approval - Jessica Patel", "body_text": "Hi HR, I'm pleased to confirm that Jessica Patel's promotion has been approved. Please process the following changes: - New Designation: Senior Software Engineer - New Salary: $145,000 - New Grade: L5 Jessica has done outstanding work this past year and truly earned this. I'd love for us to handle this thoughtfully — please make sure her manager Robert Kim is looped in before Jessica finds out through any system updates or calendar invites. I'd also like to schedule a 30-minute celebration meeting this week with Jessica, Robert, and myself to share the good news together. Once everything is in order, please send Jessica a congratulatory email. Thanks, Sarah Johnson", "body_html": "

Hi HR,

I'm pleased to confirm that Jessica Patel's promotion has been approved. Please process the following changes:

  • New Designation: Senior Software Engineer
  • New Salary: $145,000
  • New Grade: L5

Jessica has done outstanding work this past year and truly earned this. I'd love for us to handle this thoughtfully — please make sure her manager Robert Kim is looped in before Jessica finds out through any system updates or calendar invites.

I'd also like to schedule a 30-minute celebration meeting this week with Jessica, Robert, and myself to share the good news together.

Once everything is in order, please send Jessica a congratulatory email.

Thanks,
Sarah Johnson

", "timestamp": 1774040727, "cc": "", "bcc": ""}

• Designation: Senior Software Engineer
• Grade: L5
• Salary: $145,000

I've also scheduled a 30-minute celebration meeting this week (Wednesday, May 21 at 10:00 AM) with you, Sarah Johnson, and Jessica. A congratulatory email will be sent to Jessica shortly. Thanks for confirming the details!

input.subject: Congratulations on Your Well-Deserved Promotion, Jessica!
input.body:
Dear Jessica,

Congratulations on your well-deserved promotion to Senior Software Engineer! Your outstanding work over the past year has truly earned you this recognition, and we are thrilled to see your career continue to grow.

Here are your updated details:
• New Designation: Senior Software Engineer
• New Grade: L5
• New Salary: $145,000

We've scheduled a celebration meeting on Wednesday, May 21 at 10:00 AM with Robert Kim and Sarah Johnson to share in this exciting milestone together.

Once again, congratulations — we look forward to your continued success!

Best regards,
HR Team
